channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Facebook, TikTok, and YouTube EU users will soon have a new way to settle content moderation disputes
item.description: Social media users in the European Union will be able to make complaints against Facebook, ByteDance’s TikTok and Alphabet’s YouTube over content moderation to a new independent body set up in Ireland.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: U.S. judge approves FTX bankruptcy plan to repay billions
item.description: FTX received court approval of its bankruptcy plan on Monday, which will allow it to fully repay customers using up to $16.5 billion in assets recovered since the once-leading crypto exchange collapsed. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: This new device uses computer vision to teach the piano
item.description: Four out of five parents reportedly want their children to learn a musical instrument. A much smaller number of kids actually do, and even fever stick with it.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Harris backers praise Lina Khan at tech-focused fundraiser
item.description: Some Vice President Harris backers praised Federal Trade Commission (FTC) Chair Lina Khan at a tech-focused San Francisco fundraiser on Saturday headlined by former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.).
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Judge rules Google must open Android app store to competitors
item.description: A federal judge ruled Monday that Google must open the Play Store to rival third-party Android app stores. U.S. District Judge James Donato ordered the tech giant to allow for the distribution of third-party app stores and platforms through its own Android app store for a period of three years.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Unlocking the Secrets of Sports Fandom: How GenAI Revolutionizes Fan Engagement
item.description: Discover how Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) is transforming sports fandom, providing insights into fan behavior and preferences, and enabling personalized experiences for fans worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Beshad Bezhadi, Fast Company

item.title: Apple has updated iCloud.com to look shiny and new - here are all the new features you can use right now
item.description: Apple hits refresh on iCloud.com: the site has received both aesthetic and functional updates to enhance user experience.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AWS Admits Competition from On-Premises Solutions in Cloud Market
item.description: AWS acknowledges competition from on-premises IT solutions, challenging the assumption that enterprises rarely return to traditional systems once they transition to cloud computing.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cloud, edge e AI: come ottenere il massimo dalla loro integrazione
item.description: Il mondo dell’IT sta assistendo a una sempre più stretta convergenza tra cloud, edge e intelligenza artificiale.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: American Water Works cyberattack: Water supplier says its systems were hacked
item.description: The largest supplier of drinking water and wastewater services in the U.S. is the latest target to be hit by hackers.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Control the path and power of hurricanes like Milton? Forget it, scientists say
item.description: In the wake of hurricanes that have disrupted lives for millions in the U.S., some people may wonder if extreme weather events could be somehow controlled.
item.dc:creator: Melina Walling And Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A holistic approach to transitioning to a fully mature zero trust architecture
item.description: Implementing zero trust requires a significant cultural shift within all levels of an agency, as zero trust does not start and stop with IT professionals. The post A holistic approach to transitioning to a fully mature zero trust architecture first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Federal judge orders Google to open its Android app store to competition
item.description: A federal judge has ordered Google to tear down the digital walls shielding its Android app store from competition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How Helene conspiracy theories on social media are harming disaster relief efforts
item.description: In the wake of the devastation of Hurricane Helene in the United States this week, a new storm emerged on social media — false rumors about how disaster funds have been used, and even claims that officials control the weather.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: The fact that it's a phone conversation — not just in-person chit-chat — could be part of the reason people find loud speakerphone calls so grating, speculated Ozlem Ayduk.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: A new tool to help startups get a foot in the door at the Pentagon
item.description: "There are only 75 PEO offices inside the four services. And I decided to go figure out who they were," said Steve Blank. The post A new tool to help startups get a foot in the door at the Pentagon first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: Supreme Court won’t take up challenge to Jack Smith’s Trump Twitter data access
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to take up a challenge by social platform X, formerly known as Twitter, to court rulings that forced the platform to turn over data on former President Trump’s account to special counsel Jack Smith.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: iPhone stuck in SOS mode? Here's how to get out of it
item.description: There are ongoing reports of a widespread outage impacting multiple cellphone and internet providers Monday morning. According to Downdetector.com, AT&T, Verizon and T-Mobile users have all experienced issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court reinstates Indiana lawsuit against TikTok alleging child safety, privacy concerns
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — The Indiana Court of Appeals has reinstated a lawsuit filed by the state accusing TikTok of deceiving its users about the video-sharing platform's level of inappropriate content for children and the security of its consumers’ personal information.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
